observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor gives utmost importance on
presenting in the financial statements’ PPE account balances that are valid
andreliablebyrequiringtheInventoryCommitteeand theGSOto:
a. Conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and
prepare and submit the required report thereon. Investigate any discrepancies
between the inventory report and the accounting and property records and make
thenecessaryadjustmentswheretheerrorwas committed;
b. Maintain complete and updated property cards, as a counter check to the
correctnessof theaccountingrecords;
c. Prepare and maintain PAR with complete details and renew it every January of
thethirdyear afterissue or whenever thereis achangein thecustodianship; and
d. Prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at
least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities in adopting
COACircular 2020-006relative totheone-timecleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
adjust the PPE accounts, its corresponding accumulated depreciation and
governmentequityaccountto fairlypresent thebalances in thefinancial statements.

We also recommended that the Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to
follow the correct procedure on the disposal of government properties before
dropping thesamefrom thebooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated GSO to regularly
monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and its timely
renewal/registration to avoid penalties and fines as well as undue risk. Otherwise,
any surcharges, interests, penalties and expenses that may be incurred will be the
personal liability of the GSO who is responsible for the delay or non-registration of
thevehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountantto verify
from the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of P1,349,056.47 and P75,105.26
between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of
the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of the said
accountsin theMunicipality’sfinancial statement.

We recommendedthat the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
verify the accounts and if warranted draw an accounting entry to revert the
account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through a Resolution with a condition that
appropriationwill bemadeintheevent thata claim arisesinthefuture.

WerecommendedthattheMunicipal Mayor:
 Instruct the Project Development Officer to go to the DOST and ask for the
guidelinesand properutilization of thefunds;
 Requiretheresponsibledepartmentstoutilize and implementtheprojects;and
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to return the unused balance of P18,283.97 for
thecompleted projectsto thesourceagency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to submit the SORDs of the projects mentioned above to the
SAs. Henceforth, ensure that SORDs for all fund transfers are preparedandsubmitted totheSAs inaccordancewiththeCOACircular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant immediately present for audit the lacking the aforementioned BGCM transactions and practice more efficiency and economy in succeeding transactions despite its urgency in order to address the COVID-19 pandemic in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer submitted the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant:
 to exert effort to fast track and exhaust all possible means to recover the long outstanding advances to contractor projects and for the immediate recovery of the long outstanding advances to contractors;
 to make representation with the contractors to provide any of the securities mentioned in the IRR of RA No. 9184 for the above-mentioned advance payments;and
 to ensure that all subsequent advance payments are supported with the requiredsecurity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant tried to coordinated with the contractors for the security.
observations.reason: Advances to Contractor from CY 2018 and prior years, amounting to P755,877.93 were still outstanding not recouped.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality with additional source of funds to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds.
If to no avail, we recommended that the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements in filing for the request for the write-off of receivables with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already initiated a request for a write-off and the Sangguniang had already passed a resolution for the write-off of receivables. The Municipality is still in the process of completing the supporting documents required for the write-off.
observations.reason: This observation was already noted in Annual Audit Reports (AAR) 2010 - 2020, however, the Municipality is still in the process of completing the supporting documents required for the write-off of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their effort in facilitating the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor requested a total of P301,881.16 for the payment of the Estate Tax, Capital Gain Tax, Documentary Tax and Publication as an initial step to expedite the titling of the two parcels of land.
observations.reason: Lack of budget to cover the cost of titling, the Municipality could not facilitate the immediate titling of the remaining land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to prepare and submit the IIRUP and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared and submitted the IIRUP.
observations.reason: The correctness of the submitted IIRUP could not be verified since no proper inspection by two person with technical knowledge was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-30 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Designated GSO is still in the process of completing the retrieval and completion of required documents for the application of insurance to GSIS.
observations.reason: No additional property implemented was yet to be insured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:
a.to exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work/ABC of the infrastructure projects to ensure that it will not exceed the allowable/reasonable ABC; and
b.reduce the contract cost of the rehabilitation/concreting of 1.36km Anonang Burgos Farm-to-Market Road by P179,069.75. Also, require the contractor to refund the same if full payment has been made by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: In his letter dated February 7, 2022, the Municipal Engineer commented that the computation of the Approved Budget for the Contract is within the limit provided under DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016. He also cited the Bureau of Internal Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 85-2017 dated October 11, 2017 which clarified the appropriate VAT rate to be used in the preparation of ABC which is 12%.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a.Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit copies of the detailed breakdown of the ABC to the Municipal Accountant for attachment to the DVs and submission to the Audit Team to facilitate the auditorial and technical review/evaluation of the aforementioned projects; and
b.Bids and Awards Committee to:
•comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of the required documents within the prescribed period to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review/evaluation of the infrastructure projects; and
•ensure that posting of performance security by the winning bidder is prior to the signing of the Contract.
observations.managementAction: ABC was already prepared by the Municipal Engineer and was required by the Municipal Accountant to be attached to the DVs. Posting of performance security was done before the signing of the Contract.
List was already submitted by the MPDO to the Audit Team. Monthly monitoring reports were already submitted. The inclusion of necessary information on project signboards already complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
•instruct the BAC to provide an invitation and at least two other observers and strictly comply with the requirement of invitation in all stages of the procurement to be able to achieve transparency as intended by law; and
•require the BAC Secretariat to be keen in posting of documents in the PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required under RA No. 9184, to promote transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC provided at least two observers. The BAC Secretariat posted required documents to the PhilGeps website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the Certified Statement of Long Term Obligation was fully deliberated and enough amount is included in the annual budget to ensure appropriations for the settlement of the existing loan commitments. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to revisit the Annual Budget and ensure that the lacking appropriation for the disbursement of P4,850,910.59 be requested for a supplemental budget.
observations.managementAction: Details of the Certified Statement of Long Term Indebtedness were carefully deliberated and enough amount is included in the annual budget. Resolution No. 75, series of 2021 dated May 24, 2021 was issued approving Supplemental Appropriation No. 1 for the loan repayments and other related expenses amounting to P4,851,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to utilize the allocated funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to accordingly realign the funds of projects/program/activity which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021 the Municipality appropriated P34,731,795.94.
observations.reason: From the total unutilized amount of P1,335,224.23, a total of P45,569,308.71, a total of P536,194.00 of which P34,731,795.94 was already utilized as of year-end. P34,731,795.94 was appropriated, or 76 percent of the total appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO and to ensure that projects implemented under the 20 percent Development Fund will be used by the general public and will contribute to the achievement of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already demolished the structure that blocks the Municipal Street.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the establishment and the creation of the institutionalization of the LDRRMO in compliance of RA No. 10121 to ensure the safety and protection of the Municipality’s constituents during the times of disaster and emergency.
observations.managementAction: The positions essential for the institutionalization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) were created and filled. Funds were also appropriated for the operations of the said office.

observations.recommendation:
Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to make an immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the budget appropriation the funds for the titling of the said lots.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor had already requested for appropriation for the titling of land in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic in CY 2020, the request for appropriation was not prioritized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-30 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Designated GSO mentioned that due to the limited budget, the Municipality were not able to allocate funds for the insurance of various LGU properties in CY 2020. However, this CY 2021 the Municipality has already inquired from the GSIS on the application for insurance. Moreover, she affirmed that the Municipality is committed in facilitating the application for insurance of insurable LGU properties.
observations.reason: The Municipality still fails to insure properties due to budget constraint.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned BGCM transactions and practice more efficiency and
economy in succeeding transactions despite its urgency in order to address
theCOVID-19emergencyintheMunicipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committeeand theMunicipalAccountant:
 to exert effort to fast track and exhaust all possible means to submit the
information/data together with the status of these projects and for the
immediaterecoveryof thelongoutstandingadvances tocontractors;
 to make representation with the contractors to provide any of the securities
mentioned in the IRR of RA No. 9184 for the above-mentioned advance
payments; and
 to ensure that all subsequent advance payments are supported with the
requiredsecurity.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the
assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the
long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of funds to
finance itsprojects andtoavoid possiblelossof government funds.
If to no avail, we recommended that the Municipality comply with the
documentary requirements in filing for the request for write-off of receivables
with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December19,2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their effort in
facilitating the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to
avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section
148of COACircular No.92-386.
ManagementComment:
The Municipal Assessor in his letter dated March 24, 2021 mentioned that he already
requested through the Municipal Budget Office for the appropriation of land titling of the
areas for Resettlement and Public Market in the CY 2022 Annual Budget amounting to
P1,200,000.00.
He also mentioned that the Office of the Municipal Assessor together with the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office are exerting extra effort in facilitating the immediate titling of the
remaining subjectlots inthe nameoftheMunicipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to prepare and
submit the IIRUP and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the
saidpropertiesinaccordancewithapplicablerulesand regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO
to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with
appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA
Circular No. 92-30and RANo. 656.

Werecommended that theMunicipal Mayor instruct theMunicipalEngineer:
a. to exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work/ABC of the
infrastructure projects to ensure that it will not exceed the allowable/reasonable
ABC; and
b. reduce the contract cost of the rehabilitation/concreting of concreting of
1.36km Anonang Burgos Farm-to-Market Road by P179,069.75. Also,
require the contractortorefundthesameif full payment hasbeenmadebytheMunicipality.

Werecommended thattheMunicipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit copies of the detailed
breakdown of the ABC to the Municipal Accountant for attachment tothe DVs and submission to the Audit Team to facilitate the auditorial and
technicalreview/evaluationof theaforementionedprojects: and
b. BidsandAwardsCommitteeto

and technical review/evaluation of infrastructure projects; and
 ensure that posting of performance security by the winning bidder is prior to
thesigningof theContract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDO and Municipal
Engineer to:
 submit the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those that are to be
implemented during the year to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of theyear;
 inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate
project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for
validation;
 submitthemonthly monitoringreport ascurrent aspossible andupdated; and
 ensure the inclusion of all necessary information on project signboards in
compliancewiththeabove-citedcircular.

Werecommended that theMunicipal Mayor:
 instructs the BAC to provide an invitation and to at least two other
observers and strictly comply with the requirement of invitation in all
stages of the procurement to be able to achieve transparency as intended
bylaw;and
 require the BAC Secretariat to be keen in posting of documents in the
PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required under RA No. 9184,
topromotetransparencyintheprocurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to ensure that the Certified Statement of Long Term Obligation was
fully deliberated and details are properly incorporated in the annual budget to
ensure appropriationsfor thesettlement of theexistingloancommitments.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to revisit the Annual Budget and ensure that the lacking appropriation
for the disbursement of P4,850,910.59 be requested for a supplemental
budget.
Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to enforce the liquidation of all cash advances at the end of the year in compliance with COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction:
The accountable officer already liquidated the balance of P30,000.00 on January 6, 2020.
The Municipality complied with the requirement of COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of funds to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds.
If to no avail, we recommended that the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements in filing for the request for write-off of receivables with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality seek the legal assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor’s Office in favor of the Municipality in order to collect the accounts receivable through the Chairperson of Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka ng Barangay Cambuga. However, the Municipality collected P5,000.00 from its members for CY 2018.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 3, page34

The request for write off was submitted on June 9, 2020.
observations.reason: The claims against Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka ng Cambuga remain uncollected due to denial of its obligation to pay the loans as they are asserting that they have no obligation to pay. Incomplete supporting document was submitted to the Audit Team for the request for write-off, hence could not be validated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines set forth in RA No. 7160 in setting up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
• Municipal Treasurer to: submit at the beginning of the year the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; exert effort and adopt measures to ensure collections in ensuring high collection rate despite the effect of the COVID-19 pandemic; and coordinate with the Municipal Assessor for the updating of the Assessment Roll in order to prepare a reliable list of real property taxes due and collectible for each year.
observations.managementAction:
Setting-up of receivables was made at the beginning of the year by the Municipal Accountant.
Duly certified list was already submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
The Municipal Treasurer exerted effort ensuring high collection rate despite the effect of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Assessment Roll was already updated by the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to pay BAC honoraria within the collections received from the sale of bidding documents and revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund any savings realized therefrom at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made an adjustment to the books of account to comply with the DBM issuance.

observations.recommendation: Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the guidelines under DBM Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 by reverting back to the General Fund all the unused funds of previous years’ collection from sale of bidding documents.
observations.managementAction: The appropriate adjusting entry was made to reverse back to the General Fund all the unused funds of previous years’ collection from sale of bidding documents.

observations.recommendation: Werecommended that the Municipal Mayor take initiative to utilize the funds intended for the FMR project; and make representation with the Provincial Government of Quezon relative to the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Project pertaining to the funds received from the Provincial Government of Quezon has already started and the Municipality has already released the 15 percent mobilization as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:
(a) to exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work/ABC of the infrastructure projects to ensure that it will not exceed the allowable/reasonable ABC; and
(b) reduce the contract cost of the Rehabilitation/Concreting of Sitio Bagong Pook – Maralahay – Barangay Bagupaye – Anonang – Burgos Farm-to-Market Road by P237,156.07. Also, require the contractor to refund the same if full payment has been made by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has set-up receivable from the contractor and the amount of P237,156.07 will be deducted from the final payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to utilize the unexpended balance of allocated funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to accordingly realign the funds of projects/program/activity which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the balance of CY 2019 20 percent DF were utilized in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Various projects with a total appropriation of P1,335,224.23 chargeable to the Municipality’s 20 percent Development Fund continuing appropriation remain unimplemented as of year-end due to non-prioritization by the Municipality of its implementation. Reiterated in Observation No. 12, page 49.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to use the GAD Plan and Budget in the preparation of the GAD AR and to attach the documents required in Paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD programs and projects implemented during the year so that the utilization of GAD Funds be effective on addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget was used in the preparation of the GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to enforce the liquidation of all cash advances at the end of the year in
compliance with COA Circular 97-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek first the
assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the
long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of
funds to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds.

If to no avail, we recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed
with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor
and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their effort in facilitating the immediate
titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims
that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No.
92-386.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the
budget appropriation the funds for the titling of the said lots.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines set forth in RA No. 7160 in
setting up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to:
 o submit at the beginning of the year the duly certified list showing the
 name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year;
 o exert effort and adopt measures to ensure collections of the RPT/SET
 delinquencies; and
 o coordinate with the Municipal Assessor for the updating of the
 Assessment Roll in order to prepare a reliable list of real property
taxes due and collectible for each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to pay BAC honoraria within the collections received from the sale of bidding
documents for a particular period. Also, revert to the Unappropriated Surplus
of the General Fund any savings realized therefrom at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to comply with the guidelines under DBM Circular No. 2004-5A dated October
7, 2005 by reverting back to the General Fund all the unused funds of previous
years’ collection from sale of bidding documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all
the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate
insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No.
92-30 and RA No. 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take initiative to utilize the funds
intended for the FMR project; and make representation with the Provincial
Government of Quezon relative to the MOA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:
a. to exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work/ABC of the
infrastructure projects to ensure that it will not exceed the
allowable/reasonable ABC; and
b. reduce the contract cost of the Rehabilitation/Concreting of Sitio Bagong
Pook – Maralahay – Brgy Bagupaye – Anonang – Burgos Farm-to-Market
Road by P237,156.07. Also, require the contractor to refund the same if full
payment has been made by the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of funds to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds.
If to no avail, we recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality seek the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor's Office to issue demand letter in favor of the Municipality in order to collect the accounts through the Chairperson of Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka ng Barangay Cambuga. However, the Municipality collected P5,000.00 from its members for CY 2018.
We further recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
The request for write off was submitted on June 9, 2020.
observations.reason:
The claims against Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka ng Cambuga remains uncollected due to denial of its management to pay the loans as they are asserting that they have no obligation to pay.
Incomplete supporting documents was submitted to the Audit Team for the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to make an extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the budget appropriation the funds for the titling of the said lots.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has already requested for the inclusion in the budget the funds for the payment of the capital gain tax of the two untitled lots.
observations.reason: Budget for the remaining untitled lots were still not prioritized because of budget constraint.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
 submit to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year;
 inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
 submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer already submitted the list of all on-going projects/programs and activities. The Municipality had already informed the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the project or before the start of the program that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted. The Municipality submitted the monthly monitoring report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the BAC Secretariat submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts together with the supporting documents as required in accordance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in the rendering the audit.
observations.managementAction: Copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents were already furnished to the Audit Team within five working days from approval as required under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to reduce the contract cost to the immediate refund of the excess amount of P61,260.42 if full payment has been made by the Municipality. Moreover, see to it that the BIR Revenue Regulations and DPWH Department Orders were adopted in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract to avoid future reductions/refunds.
observations.managementAction: BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 85-2017 clarified that the appropriate VAT rate to be used in the preparation of the ABC is the 12 percent. The appropriate BIR Revenue Regulations and DPWH Department Orders were adopted in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the HRMO to assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive much needed job order personnel; and
 Ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the contracted personnel must be well-defined and clear in the contracts/appointments to facilitate proper evaluation of work period.
observations.managementAction:
The existing organizational structure was assessed by the HRMO and creation of plantilla position was being created.
The previous Municipal Mayor has issued Memorandum No. 2019-011 dated June 10, 2019 requiring every Department Head to issue a recommendation for the continuation/changes in the appointment (renewal) of personnel under the job order and casual status. The Memorandum also mentioned that the hiring of JOs and Contractual personnel should be based on the needs of the requesting department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) or its Secretariat and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents as presented in Annex 1 in accordance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No 2012-001 and for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in the rendering the audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has already submitted to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the MPDO in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe timeliness/proper planning that projects programmed for the intended year are implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already implemented four out of the 10 programmed projects funded under the 20 percent Development Fund from CY 2018. One project was still for implementation. Funds for the five programmed projects were augmented to other projects while one project was still for implementation.
observations.reason:
Funds for the five programmed projects were augmented to other projects while one project was still for implementation.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 10, page 42.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order personnel; Ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the contracted employees must be well-defined and clear in the contracts/appointments to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by the employees; and the hiring of job orders personnel be resorted on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds, as prescribed in Section 76 of R.A. No. 7160. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines in the hiring of personnel under Contract of Service and Job Orders Contract as provided for in CSC, COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor commented that the Municipality was compelled to engage with Contract of Service Personnel and Job Orders Contract to discharge technical functions in facilitating programs/projects from the National Government Agencies (NGAs). Majority of the present permanent personnel occupies non-technical positions and cannot suffice the technical expertise required for project implementation. It was constrained by the Personnel Services (PS) limitation and also completed the 4th tranched implementation of the salary standardization for CY 2019, hence was not able to create new plantilla positions for permanent employees. In addition thereto, by year 2020, numbers of technical positions are being proposed for creation and shall be considered in the revised Plantilla of Permanent Position. Also, with the issuance of Memorandum No. 2019-011 by the Municipal Mayor setting requirements in engaging with the Contract of Service Personnel and Job Orders Contract, the Head of Departments/Offices are enjoined to submit request with enclosed justification on the number and kind of personnel they need to hire.
observations.reason: The Audit Team duly noted the comment but it is required that the Municipality adhere to the rules and regulations of the CSC, COA and DBM in hiring of personnel. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 6 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: submit to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year; inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer commented that he only assumed office in 2019 because the former Municipal Engineer and another staff were suspended from office by the Ombudsman and they are in the process of retrieving/collating all documents/ photos of PPAs implemented by the Municipality. In addition, thereto, he informed the Audit Team that their report on PPAs was submitted to the DILG office only, hence they are retrieving documents in their files for submission to the Office of the Auditor. Nevertheless, the Municipal Engineer’s Office committed to comply with the above cited regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 3 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts together with the supporting documents within five working days from approval thereof. Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman commented that the BAC Committee are in transition period, however, committed to submit all the required reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: Despite diligent efforts, still failed to comply with the requirement due to the following reasons: Geographical distance of the Municipality to the Office of the Audit Team. Consideration of economic efficiency of submitting the documents in lump-sum and not per contract. Due to change of membership of Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), the matter was not given attention during transition. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 4 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the
assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting
the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source
of fund to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government
funds.

We further recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed
with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-
005 dated December 19, 2016 should collection be not feasible despite
diligent efforts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to make an extra effort to facilitate
the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid
contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section
148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the
budget appropriation the funds for the titling of the said lots.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
to:
 submit to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader at the
beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented
during the year;
 inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate
project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for
validation; and
 submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above
cited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts
together with the supporting documents within five working days from
approval thereof. Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with
the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting
documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
to reduce the contract cost to P2,741,721.63. Also, to cause the immediate
refund of the excess amount of P61,260.42 if full payment has been made
by the Municipality.

Moreover, see to it that the BIR Revenue Regulations and DPWH
Department Orders has been properly applied in the preparation of
Approved Budget Cost to avoid future reductions/refunds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the HRMO to assess its existing organizational structure so
that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established,
thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order
personnel;
 Ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the contracted
employees must be well-defined and clear in the
contracts/appointments to facilitate proper evaluation of work
performed by the employees; and
 the hiring of job orders personnel be resorted on necessity in order to
avoid waste of much needed funds, as prescribed in Section 76 of R.A.
No. 7160.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the
policies and guidelines in the hiring of personnel under Contract of Service
and Job Orders Contract as provided for in CSC, COA and DBM Joint
Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) or its Secretariat and the Municipal Engineer to submit to
the Audit Team all the required supporting documents as presented in
Annex 1 in accordance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No 2012-001
and for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to make an extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Five of six properties were already titled in favor of the Municipality and lot that is located at Barangay Lantangan is already on the process. The lot with Tax Declaration Nos. 46-01-453 and 46-01-448 are the same lot which is the Church Site which was already transferred to St. Apostle Parish Church as per Sangguniang Bayan Kapasiyahan Bilang 87, Taong 2016 and should be excluded from the list of untitled lots.
observations.reason: The remaining untitled lots have pending documents still being verified by the Office of Assessor. Funds for titling of the lot was included in Supplemental Budget No. 1 which was approved only on August 13, 2018 by the Sangguniang Bayan. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor seek the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor in taking legal action in collecting the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of fund to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds. We further recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 should collection be not feasible despite diligent efforts.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan had already prepared/passed a resolution for write-off of the receivables from the three debtors except the Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka since they are still in operation and earning from their business. The Municipality also seek the legal assistance from the Provincial Prosecutor’s Office to issue demand letter in favor of the Municipality in order to collect the accounts receivable thru the Chairperson of Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka ng Barangay Cambuga.
observations.reason: The SB Resolution for write-off is due for submission to Office of the Auditor. The claims against Samahan ng Nagkakaisang Magsasaka remains uncollected due to denial of its management to pay the loan as they are asserting that they have no obligation to pay. However, the Municipality collected P5,000.00 from its members for 2018. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officers to immediately liquidate/settle his cash advances within the prescribed period in compliance with existing COA regulation.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances has been liquidated at the end of the year by officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to procure only those drugs and medicines identified in the PNF Manual and issue certification that products being requisitioned and procured fall within and conform with the latest edition of the PNF Manual. Moreover, direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to ensure that all purchases of drugs and medicines are supported with the certification by the requisitioning officer or by the duly authorized officer.
observations.managementAction: The required Certification from the requisition office was already submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Henceforth, Subsequent procurement of drugs and medicines has already conformed with the latest edition of the PNF Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: COA Circular No. 92-389 has been complied by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that the Development Fund appropriated out of the Internal Revenue Allotment be fully utilized to implement mandatory on-going programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes outlined in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; install regular monitoring mechanism to assess the progress of project implementation so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom; and ensure that a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring is enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Portion of the funds were already reprogrammed to other projects such as Improvement of Water Supply System and other infrastructure projects of which the implementation is mandatory on-going to date.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor commented that since the approval from Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) for Improvement of Archaeological/Heritage Trail was not yet received, the appropriation was used to augment other project. Also, appropriations for four projects were used to augment the Construction/Opening/Gravelling of Access Road to New Government Center and to the acquisition of lot for Government Sites/Centers. In addition, the MPDC commented that the two projects namely Rehabilitation of Multi-Purpose Hall at PUP and Construction of Access Road to Slaughterhouse were already completed. Other appropriations are for reprogramming in the ensuing year. Reiteration in Part II, Observation No. 9 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period in order for the Government to utilize the same and free the officials concerned of any liability in terms of surcharges that may be imposed by BIR.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld was already remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to see to it that projects under the 20% development fund are fully implemented to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes envisioned under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part ll, Observation No.3.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
   Assessor to expedite the titling of the ten (10) parcels of lot to  avoid
   third party claims in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek the assistance of the
   Provincial Prosecutor in taking  legal  action  in  collecting  the  long
   overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional source of fund
   to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Engineer to see to it that projects under the 20%  Development  Fund  are
   fully implemented to achieve the desired socio-economic  development  and
   environmental management outcomes envisioned under JMC No. 2011-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  the  counsel  for
   the Municipality to make representation with  the  Construction  Industry
   Arbitration Commission to expedite the resolution of the dispute so  that
   appropriate action could be taken thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to expedite the determination of the status of these properties for possible transfer to the Department of Education of lots used as school sites and facilitate the titling of the remaining lots owned/used by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The necessary documents for the titling of lot was submitted to DENR, while titling of those being used by DepEd as school sites were already transferred to DepEd officials through SB Resolution No. 87 dated August 8, 2016.
observations.reason: Validation of documents by appropriate government agency is still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer adopt a more aggressive collection strategy and if necessary, take legal action in collecting the long overdue receivables to provide the Municipality additional sources to finance its projects and to avoid possible loss of government funds. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts in locating the documents relative to the previous request for write-off. Otherwise, submit another request to the Commission on Audit, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent collection letters through a private lawyer at her own expense. The SB Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to request for the write-off of the loan granted to the Samahan ng Maliliit na Magniniyog in the amount of P180,000.00 was already submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The NGOs/POs has yet to respond. The SB Resolution is to be amended considering that the amount indicated therein differs from the recorded amount being requested for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to demand the immediate liquidation of the cash advance, in compliance with existing regulation. Failure to liquidate by the Accountable Officer, deduct the corresponding amount from the money due the accountable officer. Henceforth, see to it that advances are liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance was refunded under OR No. 3215458 dated July 12, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to provide cash back-up to the cash deficit before making any disbursements for current obligations.
observations.managementAction: The cash deficit was fully recovered as of year-end. The cash in the amount of P2,193,290.03 was more than enough to cover the obligations in the amount of P1,078,459.54.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer/Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection of completed and duly accepted projects and prepare and submit the Inspection Reports to ensure that the construction materials were put into its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects implemented by Administration are now being supported with Inspection Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Auditor: At the beginning of the year the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year; and The monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The list of on-going projects and those to be implemented for CY 2017 was already submitted. The report for the 1st quarter of 2017 was already submitted.
